title: dactylis glomerata subsp. aschersoniana
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. aschersoniana, also known as Ascherson's cock's foot, is a subspecies of the dactylis glomerata plant, a type of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Dactylis glomerata subsp. aschersoniana can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a versatile herb in herbal medicine.
